liberal.definition: One with liberal views, supporting individual liberty (see Wikipedia's article on Liberalism).

liberation.definition: The act of liberating or the state of being liberated.

liberty.definition: The condition of being free from control or restrictions.

lifelong.definition: Extending for the entire duration of life.

likelihood.definition: The probability of a specified outcome; the chance of something happening; probability; the state or degree of being probable.

limb.definition: A major appendage of human or animal, used for locomotion (such as an arm, leg or wing).

linear.definition: Having the form of a line; straight or roughly straight; following a direct course.

linger.definition: To stay or remain in a place or situation, especially as if unwilling to depart or not easily able to do so; to loiter.

listing.definition: To create or recite a list.

litre.definition: The metric unit of fluid measure, equal to one cubic decimetre. Symbols: l, L, ℓ

literacy.definition: The ability to read and write.

liver.definition: A large organ in the body that stores and metabolizes nutrients, destroys toxins and produces bile. It is responsible for thousands of biochemical reactions.

lobby.definition: An entryway or reception area; vestibule; passageway; corridor.

log.definition: The trunk of a dead tree, cleared of branches.

logic.definition: A method of human thought that involves thinking in a linear, step-by-step manner about how a problem can be solved. Logic is the basis of many principles including the scientific method.

loom.definition: A utensil; tool; a weapon; (usually in compound) an article in general.

loop.definition: A length of thread, line or rope that is doubled over to make an opening.

loyalty.definition: The state of being loyal; fidelity.

machinery.definition: The machines constituting a production apparatus, in a plant etc., collectively.

magnetic.definition: Of, relating to, operating by, or caused by magnetism.

magnitude.definition: The absolute or relative size, extent or importance of something.

mainland.definition: The continent; the principal land, as distinguished from islands or a peninsula.

mainstream.definition: The principal current in a flow, such as a river or flow of air

maintenance.definition: Actions performed to keep some machine or system functioning or in service.

mandate.definition: An official or authoritative command; an order or injunction; a commission; a judicial precept.

mandatory.definition: A sign or line that require the path of the disc to be above, below or to one side of it.

manifest.definition: A list or invoice of the passengers or goods being carried by a commercial vehicle or ship.

manipulate.definition: To move, arrange or operate something using the hands

manipulation.definition: The practice of manipulating or the state of being manipulated.

manuscript.definition: A book, composition or any other document, written by hand (or manually typewritten), not mechanically reproduced.

march.definition: A formal, rhythmic way of walking, used especially by soldiers, bands and in ceremonies.

marine.definition: A soldier, normally a member of a marine corps, trained to serve on board or from a ship

marketplace.definition: An open area in a town housing a public market.

mask.definition: A cover, or partial cover, for the face, used for disguise or protection.

massacre.definition: The killing of a considerable number (usually limited to people) where little or no resistance can be made, with indiscriminate violence, without necessity, and contrary to civilized norms.

mature.definition: Fully developed; grown up in terms of physical appearance, behaviour or thinking; ripe.

maximise.definition: To make as large as possible

meantime.definition: The time spent waiting for another event; time in between.

medieval.definition: Someone living in the Middle Ages.

meditation.definition: A devotional exercise of, or leading to contemplation.

melody.definition: Tune; sequence of notes that makes up a musical phrase

memoir.definition: An autobiography; a book describing the personal experiences of an author.

mentor.definition: A wise and trusted counselor or teacher

merchant.definition: A person who traffics in commodities for profit.

mercy.definition: Relenting; forbearance to cause or allow harm to another.

mere.definition: A body of standing water, such as a lake or a pond. More specifically, it can refer to a lake that is broad in relation to its depth. Also included in place names such as Windermere.

merge.definition: The joining together of multiple sources.

merit.definition: A claim to commendation or a reward.

methodology.definition: (originally science) The study of methods used in a field.

midst.definition: A place in the middle of something; may be used of a literal or metaphorical location.

migration.definition: An instance of moving to live in another place for a while.

militia.definition: An army of trained civilians, which may be an official reserve army, called upon in time of need, the entire able-bodied population of a state which may also be called upon, or a private force not under government control.

mill.definition: A grinding apparatus for substances such as grains, seeds, etc.

minimal.definition: The smallest possible amount, quantity, or degree.

minimise.definition: To make (something) as small or as insignificant as possible.

ministry.definition: Government department, at the administrative level normally headed by a minister (or equivalent rank, e.g. secretary of state), who holds it as portfolio, especially in a constitutional monarchy, but also as a polity

minute.definition: A unit of time equal to sixty seconds (one-sixtieth of an hour).

miracle.definition: An event that appears inexplicable by the laws of nature and so is held to be supernatural in origin.

misery.definition: Great unhappiness; extreme pain of body or mind; wretchedness; distress; woe.

misleading.definition: To lead astray, in a false direction.

missile.definition: Any object used as a weapon by being thrown or fired through the air, such as stone, arrow or bullet.

mob.definition: A large or disorderly group of people; especially one bent on riotous or destructive action.

mobile.definition: A kinetic sculpture or decorative arrangement made of items hanging so that they can move independently from each other.

mobility.definition: The ability to move; capacity for movement.

moderate.definition: One who holds an intermediate position between extremes, as in politics.

modification.definition: The form of existence belonging to a particular object, entity etc.; a mode of being.

module.definition: A self-contained component of a system, often interchangeable, which has a well-defined interface to the other components.

momentum.definition: Of a body in motion: the tendency of a body to maintain its inertial motion; the product of its mass and velocity.

monk.definition: A male member of a monastic order who has devoted his life for religious service.

monopoly.definition: A situation, by legal privilege or other agreement, in which solely one party (company, cartel etc.) exclusively provides a particular product or service, dominating that market and generally exerting powerful control over it.

morality.definition: Recognition of the distinction between good and evil or between right and wrong; respect for and obedience to the rules of right conduct; the mental disposition or characteristic of behaving in a manner intended to produce morally good results.

motive.definition: An idea or communication that makes one want to act, especially from spiritual sources; a divine prompting.

municipal.definition: A financial instrument issued by a municipality.

naval.definition: Of or relating to a navy.

neighbouring.definition: Situated or living nearby or adjacent to.

nest.definition: A structure built by a bird as a place to incubate eggs and rear young.

newsletter.definition: A periodically sent publication containing current events or the like, generally on a particular topic or geared toward a limited audience.

niche.definition: A cavity, hollow, or recess, generally within the thickness of a wall, for a statue, bust, or other erect ornament. Hence, any similar position, literal or figurative.

noble.definition: An aristocrat; one of aristocratic blood.

nod.definition: An instance of inclining the head up and down, as to indicate agreement, or as a cursory greeting.

nominate.definition: To name someone as a candidate for a particular role or position, including that of an office.

nomination.definition: An act or instance of nominating.
